title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: The marks we use to organize written language are often (0) taken for granted, yet punctuation has a long and fascinating history. In ancient times, texts were often written without any spaces or marks at all, a style (1) as scriptuo continua. This made reading a slow and (2) task, usually reserved for those who could read aloud. It was not until the arrival of the printing press that punctuation began to (3) into the system we recognize today. Early printers needed a way to (4) consistency across their publications, leading to the standardization of commas, periods, and question marks. These symbols (5) a vital function by indicating pauses and clarifying the relationship between different parts of a sentence. Over time, punctuation has continued to (6) in response to new forms of communication. For example, the rise of digital messaging has seen the (7) of emojis and unconventional punctuation to convey tone and emotion. While some traditionalists (8) these changes as a decline in linguistic standards, others see them as a natural development of a living language that must adapt to the needs of its users in an increasingly fast-paced and interconnected world of global digital exchange.

example.explanation: “Taken for granted” is a fixed idiom meaning to fail to appreciate something because it is so familiar.

questions.explanation: “Known as” is the standard way to introduce a specific term or name.
“Laborious” is a formal adjective for a task that requires a lot of time and effort.
“Evolve” is used here to describe the gradual development of a system over time.
“Ensure consistency” is a common formal collocation meaning to make sure something stays the same.
“Perform a function” is the standard collocation for describing the purpose of a tool or symbol.
“Shift” in this context means to change position or nature in response to external factors.
“Emergence” is a formal term for the process of something becoming visible or prominent.
“View these changes as” is a common structure for expressing an opinion or perspective.
